title: Interpret Area-To-Area Movement Using The Layout Entrance And Exit Model
summary: Use the documented modular layout model to interpret how an AGB/AGV moves between named areas. The source explains that distinct areas are connected through entrance and exit points, and movement should be understood as exiting one area before entering the next area.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing or explaining the OptiSweep layout model for area-to-area movement, identifying named areas in the example design, or describing how an AGB/AGV transitions between modular areas such as Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper.
not_for: Any procedure requiring more detailed pathing or routing than the source provides

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Use the layout view and source terminology to identify areas and explain movement using the entrance-and-exit model.

safety_notes: This source provides a layout interpretation model only, not a control or recovery procedure.
Stop if a more detailed pathing, routing, or system-control procedure is needed because it is not provided in this source.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the layout slide or documented layout view
Knowledge of the source-named area labels

access_required: Access to the training layout slide or documented layout representation
system_preconditions: The user can view the named layout areas shown in the source

visual_references.description: Training slide/frame showing the modular layout concept, common entrance and exit model, and named areas including Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper.

steps.title: Locate the layout representation
steps.purpose: Establish the source view that defines the modular area model and named layout areas.
steps.instruction: Open or reference the layout representation from the source that shows the named areas and their connections. Use the slide or frame that presents the modular area arrangement and the common entrance and exit concept.
steps.expected_result: The relevant layout view is available for interpretation.
steps.healthy_condition: The layout view shows distinct named areas and indicates the entrance-and-exit-based modular arrangement.
steps.failure_condition: The user does not have the source layout view or cannot identify the modular area representation.

actions.label: Open layout slide or frame
actions.exact_action: Locate the layout representation showing the named areas and their connections.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the modular layout explanation and the named areas Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper, along with the common entrance and exit concept.

stop_or_escalate_if: The layout slide or documented layout view is not available
A more detailed routing or control procedure is required

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The layout is designed for modularity and scalability, with distinct areas connected by a common entrance and exit.
Training slide and discussion segment explaining the modular OptiSweep facility layout and how AGVs move between areas such as hospitals, sorter sides, chargers, and tippers.

steps.title: Identify the current and destination areas
steps.purpose: Define the movement being interpreted using the source-named area types.
steps.instruction: Identify the current area and the destination area using the source terminology shown in the layout, such as Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, or Tipper.
steps.expected_result: The movement is framed between two named areas from the source layout.
steps.healthy_condition: Both the current area and destination area can be named using source terminology.
steps.failure_condition: One or both areas cannot be matched to the source-named layout areas.

actions.label: Identify named areas
actions.exact_action: Identify the current area and the destination area using the source-named area types such as Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, or Tipper.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the area labels Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper in the example layout.

stop_or_escalate_if: The area names in use do not match the source layout terminology
The user needs site-specific routing detail not present in this source

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR lists areas: Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, Tipper; narration says the layout has hospitals, sorter sides, chargers, and tippers.

steps.title: Verify the exit-then-entrance movement rule
steps.purpose: Apply the source-defined rule for interpreting movement between modular areas.
steps.instruction: Verify that the movement being interpreted follows the documented rule that an AGB/AGV goes through an exit before entering the next area.
steps.expected_result: The movement is understood according to the documented modular transition rule.
steps.healthy_condition: The interpreted movement includes leaving one area through its exit and then entering the next area.
steps.failure_condition: The movement is being interpreted as direct crossing from one area to another without the exit-before-entry model.

actions.label: Apply movement rule
actions.exact_action: Verify that movement between areas follows the documented rule that an AGB goes through an exit before entering the next area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Look for the statement or depiction that distinct areas are connected by a common entrance and exit and that the vehicle must exit one area before entering the next.

stop_or_escalate_if: A detailed route sequence or control action is needed beyond the source explanation
The movement cannot be interpreted using the exit-before-entry model shown in the source

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: To move from one area to another, an AGV must go to the exit before entering the next.

steps.title: Describe the path using exit then entrance
steps.purpose: Ensure explanations of movement match the source model rather than implying unsupported direct crossing behavior.
steps.instruction: When tracing or explaining a path, describe the movement as an area exit followed by the next area entrance, rather than as a direct area-to-area crossing.
steps.expected_result: The path explanation matches the source-defined modular layout model.
steps.healthy_condition: The explanation uses exit-from-area and enter-next-area wording.
steps.failure_condition: The explanation implies unsupported direct crossing or routing detail not stated in the source.

actions.label: State movement correctly
actions.exact_action: When tracing or explaining a path, describe the movement as area exit followed by next area entrance rather than direct area-to-area crossing.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the modular layout slide as the visual basis for describing movement between areas.

stop_or_escalate_if: Someone requests exact routing logic, queue logic, or control behavior not provided in this source

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Distinct areas are connected by a common entrance and exit. To move from one area to another, an AGV must go to the exit before entering the next.

steps.title: Record or communicate the area names
steps.purpose: Preserve consistent terminology when documenting or discussing movement.
steps.instruction: Record or communicate the relevant area names involved in the movement using the source terminology from the layout.
steps.expected_result: The movement description uses consistent source-based area names.
steps.healthy_condition: Area names are documented or communicated using terms such as Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper when applicable.
steps.failure_condition: Area names are omitted, inconsistent, or replaced with unsupported terminology.

actions.label: Use source terminology
actions.exact_action: Record or communicate the relevant area names involved in the movement using the source terminology.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference the displayed area labels so the communicated names match the source.

stop_or_escalate_if: The required terminology is site-specific and not shown in this source
The user needs a formal routing or control procedure rather than a terminology-based interpretation

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR lists areas: Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, Tipper; narration says the layout has hospitals, sorter sides, chargers, and tippers.

success_criteria: The user can identify named areas in the source layout.
The user explains movement between areas using the exit-before-entry model.
The user communicates area names using source terminology.
healthy_conditions: The layout is interpreted as modular and scalable with distinct areas.
Movement is described as exiting one area before entering the next.
Named areas such as Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper are used consistently.
failure_conditions: The user attempts to infer detailed routing logic not provided by the source.
Movement is described as direct area-to-area crossing without exit and entrance transitions.
The source layout view or area labels are unavailable.
escalation_guidance: Escalate or stop when more detailed pathing, routing, or system-control procedure is needed because this source does not provide it.
Escalate if site-specific movement behavior must be confirmed beyond the training layout model.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Modular layout slide/frame showing common entrance and exit behavior and named areas Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, and Tipper.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The layout is designed for modularity and scalability, with distinct areas connected by a common entrance and exit. To move from one area to another, an AGV must go to the exit before entering the next.
OCR lists areas: Hospital, SorterSide, Charger, Tipper; narration says the layout has hospitals, sorter sides, chargers, and tippers.

missing_details: The source does not provide detailed routing logic between specific areas.
The source does not provide control actions, commands, or system workflow steps for moving an AGB/AGV.
The source packet uses both AGB and AGV terminology without resolving the distinction.
No time estimate is provided in the source.
